title: Trump Wants a Quick Victory in Iran. But the War May Be Costly.
summary: The opening days of the conflict are challenging the idea that President Trump can project force abroad while safeguarding American lives and the economy.

title: On the Ground in Beirut
summary: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s military seizes areas of southern Lebanon and carries out bombings.

title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
summary: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.

title: How War in the Persian Gulf Could Spill Into the U.S. Economy
summary: Rising energy prices, snarled supply chains and higher government debt could all hurt American consumers.

title: Hegseth Says War Will Go On ‘Until We Decide,’ and U.S. Court Moves Toward Ordering Tariff Refunds
summary: Plus, why you should not let A.I. do your taxes.

title: What to Know About the Texas Runoffs in May: Key Races and Candidates
summary: Texas voters will revisit the Republican Senate primary — and some House races where no candidate captured more than 50 percent of the vote — in runoffs on May 26.

title: Primaries Offer Warning Signs to Incumbents in Both Parties
summary: Even as some top targets held on, lawmakers in both parties were pushed into runoffs by challengers in Texas, while some in the North Carolina state legislature lost.

title: F.D.A. Faces Upset Over Denials of New Drugs
summary: Agency officials promise fast reviews of new treatments while vowing they will not be a “rubber stamp” for the industry. But patients with rare diseases view recent decisions as signs that the doors are closing on their options.

title: In a Riskier Era, China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
summary: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western technology.

title: China Sets Economy’s Growth Target Below 5% for First Time in Decades
summary: The goal, announced at a gathering of Communist Party leaders, was the lowest since 1991 and can offer clues about China’s policymaking plans.

title: What the Extraordinary Market Volatility in Asia Says About Energy and A.I.
summary: Stocks in South Korea and Taiwan, the center of global chip making, plunged on fears about energy prices. Their recovery shows the bullishness over artificial intelligence.

title: They Made It Through the 24 Hours That Rocked South Korea’s Markets
summary: After explosive growth in recent months, the country’s benchmark stock index plummeted, then rebounded, in the wake of the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran.

title: Harry Styles Left as a Dominant Male Pop Star. He Returns to a Crowd.
summary: In the four years since the British singer last released an album, artists like Sombr, working in similar aesthetic modes, have climbed onto the charts.

title: The Long March of Presidential Power That Led to War on Iran
summary: Presidents have sidestepped Congress to launch limited military strikes for decades. Trump’s decision to attack Iran is an aggressive escalation.

title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
summary: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.

title: James Luckey-Lange Recounts Being Detained, Starved and Beaten in Venezuela
summary: James Luckey-Lange, 28, wrote about kindness and shared humanity as he traveled. But he said he had been shackled, starved and beaten in Venezuela after being detained.

title: ‘No Tax on Overtime’ Rule Isn’t What Some Workers Were Expecting
summary: President Trump’s simple campaign promise, now reality, includes lots of fine print that is confounding taxpayers and companies this filing season.

title: A Word to the Wise: Don’t Trust A.I. to File Your Taxes
summary: The world’s smartest technology is no match for the U.S. tax code.

title: Why Does Child Care Seem Less Affordable Than Ever?
summary: It has always been expensive, but recently prices have risen faster than inflation.

title: $2.5 Million Rift Pits Cannabis Pioneer Against Group That Backed Her
summary: A dispute between the nonprofit Housing Works and the owner of a cannabis dispensary shows the risks for businesses in an industry that is locked out of traditional financing and resources.

title: A Dog-Sledding Adventure in Northern Minnesota
summary: On the edge of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area, a novice learns to drive her own team on an adventure in the heart of winter.

title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
summary: How the antiwar right sees Trump’s foreign policy.

title: Your Job May Already Be in Jeopardy
summary: Michael Steinberger on how A.I.’s impact on white-collar jobs may transform politics and society.

title: Late Night Doesn’t Understand Why America Is Attacking Iran
summary: “This could be the first war ever launched based on vibes,” Jimmy Fallon said.

title: After Gen-Z Revolution, Nepal Votes for a Fresh Slate of Leaders
summary: A millennial rapper and former mayor is aiming to lead the country, six months after the old guard was ousted.

title: Gonzales Admits Affair as Republicans Move to Censure Him
summary: The day after being forced into a runoff to keep his seat, Representative Tony Gonzales confirmed that he had an extramarital affair with an aide who later took her own life.

title: Juan Jose Valdez, Last Marine Out of Saigon, Dies at 88
summary: During the fall of the South Vietnamese capital in 1975, he helped evacuate thousands, and was nearly left behind.

title: Ethiopia’s Demand for a Port Drives Fear of New War With Eritrea
summary: Both sides have moved troops toward their shared border, while Ethiopia has accused Eritrea of occupying part of its territory.
